question: Name the longest-running shows in Broadway history.

answers.display: The Phantom of the Opera — 13,981 performances (1988–2023)

factCheck: Chicago and Lion King are still running; verify current counts before play.

question: Name the Broadway musicals that have won the most Tony Awards.

answers.display: The Producers — 12 Tony wins (2001, all-time record)
The Book of Mormon — 9 Tony wins (2011)

factCheck: Hello, Dolly! and Billy Elliot are tied at 10 wins.

question: Name the highest-grossing Broadway shows of all time.

answers.display: The Lion King — $2 billion+ in total gross
The Phantom of the Opera — $1.3 billion+ in total gross

factCheck: Gross figures accumulate as long-running shows continue; verify before play.

question: Name the individuals with the most Tony Award wins in Broadway history.

answers.display: Audra McDonald — 6 Tony wins (most by any performer)

factCheck: Sondheim and Fosse are tied at 8; verify exact Tommy Tune total.

question: Name the Broadway shows with the most Tony Award nominations.

answers.display: The Book of Mormon — 14 Tony nominations (2011)

factCheck: The Producers and Billy Elliot are tied at 15; Moulin Rouge! and Book of Mormon are tied at 14.

question: Name the longest-running shows in West End history.

answers.display: The Phantom of the Opera — ~13,500 performances (1986–2020)

factCheck: The Mousetrap is still running and adds performances every year.

question: Name the performers with the most Tony Award wins for acting.

answers.display: Audra McDonald — 6 acting Tonys (all-time record for any performer)

factCheck: Angela Lansbury also received a special Tony, bringing her total to 5. Peters and LuPone are tied at 2 competitive wins.

question: Name the playwrights with the most Pulitzer Prizes for Drama.

answers.display: Eugene O'Neill — 4 Pulitzer Prizes for Drama
Edward Albee — 3 Pulitzer Prizes for Drama
August Wilson — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Drama (Fences, The Piano Lesson)
Tennessee Williams — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Drama (Streetcar Named Desire, Cat on a Hot Tin Roof)
Lynn Nottage — 2 Pulitzer Prizes for Drama (Ruined, Sweat)

factCheck: Several playwrights share 2 Pulitzers; August Wilson, Tennessee Williams, Lynn Nottage, and others all have 2.

question: Name the stage musicals whose film adaptations have grossed the most at the worldwide box office.

answers.display: The Sound of Music (1965) — $286 million worldwide (over $2B inflation-adjusted)

factCheck: Unadjusted box office figures used; The Sound of Music would rank #1 if inflation-adjusted.

question: Name the performers with the most Tony Award nominations.

answers.display: Chita Rivera — 10 Tony nominations (most by any performer)

factCheck: Verify Angela Lansbury nomination count; Peters and LuPone may be tied.
